With Knicks fans purchasing huge amount of tickets for tonight's game, the Spurs have restricted ticket sales for tonight's game.

Only customers within 150 miles of Frost Bank Center (by billing address) can buy.

Out-of-area purchases may be cancelled & refunded.

items.title: An NBA first: Introducing standard errors for box-score metrics
items.author: 5x5
items.body_excerpt: Believe it or not, your favorite advanced NBA metric — whether BPM, EPM, LEBRON, DARKO, Net Points or my own xRAPM — is uncertain about the estimates it spits out. In the stats world, measures of uncertainty are known as standard errors and confidence intervals . Why would an…

items.author: J.E. Skeets
items.body_excerpt:
The Knicks are looking to become the first team since the Bucks in '21 to win a title WITHOUT a Canadian on the roster

Andrew Wiggins (Warriors in '22)
Jamal Murray (Nuggets in '23)
Oshae Brissett (Celtics in '24)
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander & Lu Dort (Thunder in '25)

Maybe OG should count? 😉

items.title: The Spurs scored 76 points in the first half of Game 4. That's the second-most points ever scored in the first half of an NBA Finals game.
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
The Spurs scored 76 points in the first half of Game 4. That's the second-most points ever scored in the first half of an NBA Finals game. 

The Spurs scored 30 points in the second half of Game 4. That's the fewest points ever scored in the second half of an NBA Finals game.

items.title: I’m all about Balogun playing for the US team via birthright citizenship and I’m super all about birthright citizenship, but it is kind of on the nose RE: our youth soccer system that yesterday’s star player was only tarnished by one month in the US as a child.

items.title: Hollander POTD Sarunas Marciulionis. Lithuanian great was hard charging SG drafted 127th in 87 by GS. Could score and got to the line a ton. Peaked at 19 ppg in 91-92 on .538 FG%. Hurt in 93 + was only bench player for a few years after. Retired in 97 at age 32. Made HOF.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollander POTD Sarunas Marciulionis. Lithuanian great was hard charging SG drafted 127th in 87 by GS. Could score and got to the line a ton. Peaked at 19 ppg in 91-92 on .538 FG%. Hurt in 93 + was only bench player for a few years after. Retired in 97 at age 32. Made HOF.
